headline: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
description: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.

headline: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
Gaza's children, traumatized and mutilated by war: 'His prosthesis hurts, but he endures it to show that he isn't different'
description: Due to the bombings carried out by Israel since October 7, 2023, the Gaza Strip now holds the grim record for the highest number of amputee children per capita in the world, according to the World Health Organization. These children have been trying to rebuild their lives despite forced displacement and lack of medical care in a devastated territory.

headline: 'Netanyahu still regards the 1946 King David Hotel bombers in Jerusalem as heroes'
description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has never stopped defending the attack, which claimed the highest-ever number of civilian lives in British Mandate Palestine, historian Jean-Pierre Filiu writes in his column.

headline: Alexander Skarsgård, a daring actor
description: The 49-year-old Swedish actor plays a gay, dominant biker in 'Pillion.' It's an unexpected role for Skarsgård, who is known for Hollywood blockbusters but is determined not to be typecast as a conventional heartthrob.

headline: Is the Bible terrifying? Three frightening stories from the holy book
description: The biblical corpus shared by Judaism and Christianity contains scenes of extraordinary violence, sometimes embodied by terrifying figures. For centuries, such passages have puzzled scholars and fascinated readers.

19 men charged in mass rapes case among 'libertines' in France
description: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'

headline: BTS, the South Korean group that propelled K-pop to the top of the global music industry
description: As the seven artists perform on July 17 and 18 at the Stade de France, we look back at their beginnings in 2013 and their unprecedented rise.

headline: Why Russia's hybrid warfare is forcing France to raise its voice
description: Between acts of sabotage and drone flyovers, the General Directorate of Internal Security (DGSI) attributed a large-scale cyberespionage campaign to Russian domestic intelligence. In response, the chargé d'affaires at the Russian embassy in Paris was summoned by the foreign minister on Friday, July 17.
